19 January 2026 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • Minnesota National Guard mobilized as tensions persist in U.S. Minneapolis People's Daily 19 Jan 2026 -- The Minnesota National Guard has been mobilized as tensions remain high in Minneapolis, the largest city in the U.S. state, authorities said on Saturday.
  • U.S. Army puts 1,500 paratroopers on alert for possible deployment to Minnesota: media People's Daily 19 Jan 2026 -- Some 1,500 active-duty U.S. Army paratroopers have been placed on alert for a potential deployment to the state of Minnesota, U.S. media reported on Sunday, citing defense officials.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Trump says 'feels no obligation' to pursue peace after Nobel Prize snub Press TV 19 Jan 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has told Norway's prime minister that he no longer feels obliged to focus on world peace after failing to receive the Nobel Peace Prize.

  • Trump Says Not Commenting on Readiness to Resort to Use of Force to Seize Greenland Sputnik 19 Jan 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said on Monday that he is not commenting on his readiness to resort to the use of force to seize Greenland.
  • Foreign Secretary Statement to the House on Greenland and wider Arctic security UK FCDO 19 Jan 2026 -- Foreign Secretary Statement to the House regarding Greenland and wider Arctic security.
  • Canada Considering Sending Troops to Greenland - Reports Sputnik 19 Jan 2026 -- Amid US President Donald Trump's claims to Greenland, Canadian authorities have developed plans to send a small contingent of troops to the island for exercises with other NATO members, The Globe and Mail reported citing two senior Canadian government officials.
  • EU Seeks to 'Engage, Not Escalate' With US Over Greenland, Avoid New Tariffs Sputnik 19 Jan 2026 -- The European Union is seeking to "engage, not escalate" with the United States when it comes to Washington's desire to control Greenland in order to avoid new US trade tariffs, European Commission spokesman Olof Gill said on Monday.
  • Trump Reveals Real Reason for Greenland Grab: Payback for Nobel Prize Snub Sputnik 19 Jan 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has confirmed in a letter to Norwegian Prime Minister Jonas Gahr Store that his demand for control of Greenland stems from the Norwegian Nobel Committee's refusal to award him the Nobel Peace Prize, PBS journalist Nick Schifrin said on Monday, citing the letter.
  • Russia Special Presidential Envoy Dmitriev Says EU's Quick 'Surrender' to US Goes as Expected Sputnik 19 Jan 2026 -- Kirill Dmitriev, head of the Russian Direct Investment Fund (RDIF) and special presidential envoy, said on Monday that the European Union had quickly conceded to the United States following reports that the EU does not yet plan to introduce countermeasures against the US over the situation with Greenland.
  • Foreign Minister Tsahkna to EU counterparts: Estonia stands firmly for Denmark's territorial integrity and sovereignty Estonia MFA 19 Jan 2026 -- In a phone call today, 19 January, with his European Union counterparts and the EU High Representative for Foreign Affairs and Security Policy, Kaja Kallas, Foreign Minister Margus Tsahkna said that Estonia stands firmly for Denmark's territorial integrity and sovereignty and is committed to developing further joint steps by the EU.
  • US urged to stop using so-called 'China-threat' as pretext to pursue selfish interests, says FM on Trump's additional tariff on 8 European countries for Greenland deal Global Times 19 Jan 2026 -- China has repeatedly elaborated its position on the Greenland issue on many occasions. International law, based on the purposes and principles of the UN Charter, is the foundation of the current international order and must be upheld. We urge the US side to stop using the so-called "China threat" as a pretext to pursue selfish interests, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said.
  • EU ready to defend itself against any coercion: European Council President Global Times 19 Jan 2026 -- European Council President Antonio Costa said Sunday on X that the European Union (EU) is "ready to defend itself against any form of coercion" in response to U.S. President Donald Trump's tariff threats over Greenland.
  • Trump's tariffs threat over Greenland sparks EU pushback People's Daily 19 Jan 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump's threat to impose new tariffs on eight European countries unless Denmark accept a deal over Greenland has drawn swift rebukes from Nordic capitals and major European leaders, fueled calls for an EU-level response, and cast fresh doubt over the implementation of last year's EU-U.S. tariff "truce."
  • Trump threatens 10% tariffs on eight NATO allies over Greenland tensions People's Daily 19 Jan 2026 -- US President Donald Trump on Saturday threatened to impose a 10 percent tariff on eight NATO allies, including Denmark, Norway, Sweden, France, Germany, the United Kingdom, the Netherlands and Finland, in response to recent military activity in Greenland.
  • European countries rally against U.S. tariff over Greenland People's Daily 19 Jan 2026 -- The transatlantic rift over Greenland deepened remarkably on Sunday as Europe mounted a rare, unified rebuke to punitive tariff threats from U.S. President Donald Trump.

  • 'Most visible' NATO exercise in 2026, STEADFAST DART, begins NATO SHAPE 19 Jan 2026 -- Allied Command Operations (ACO) officially launched NATO exercise Steadfast Dart 2026 across Central Europe, Jan. 15.
  • NATO Secretary General hosts Denmark's Defence Minister and Greenland's Foreign Minister at NATO HQ NATO 19 Jan 2026 -- On Monday 19 January 2026, the Defence Minister of the Kingdom of Denmark, Troels Lund Poulsen, and the Foreign Minister of Greenland, Vivian Motzfeldt, visited NATO headquarters in Brussels, Belgium, for a bilateral meeting with Secretary General Mark Rutte.
  • SACEUR's visit to Romania Romania - Ministry of National Defence 19 Jan 2026 -- General Gheorghiță Vlad, the Chief of Defence Staff, and General Alexus G. Grynkewich, Supreme Allied Commander Europe (SACEUR) and Commander of the US European Command (USEUCOM), will pay a working visit to the "Getica" Joint National Training Center in Cincu and to Headquarters Multinational Corps Southeast (HQ MNC-SE) in Sibiu, on Tuesday, January 20th.

  • Rosoboronexport Inks Several Export Contracts for Kamikaze Drones Sputnik 19 Jan 2026 -- Rosoboronexport, Russia's defense export agency, has already signed a number of export contracts for the delivery of unmanned aerial vehicles and kamikaze drones, CEO Alexander Mikheev announced on the eve of the UMEX and SimTEX 2026 exhibition in the UAE.

  • Deputy Minister of Defence Sergiy Boyev and Speaker of the Latvian Saeima Daiga Mieriņa discuss bilateral military cooperation in 2026 Ukraine MOD 19 Jan 2026
  • Ministry of Defence outlines directions for the development of Support Services of the Armed Forces of Ukraine Ukraine MOD 19 Jan 2026
  • Command of the 1st Azov NGU Corps transitions to digital logistics management Ukraine MOD 19 Jan 2026
  • Mykhailo Fedorov and John Healey discuss data sharing and joint defence projects Ukraine MOD 19 Jan 2026
  • Minister of Defence signs order appointing Colonel Pavlo Yelizarov as Deputy Commander of the Air Force Ukraine MOD 19 Jan 2026

  • Wargamers explore tech of tomorrow Australia DOD 19 Jan 2026 -- Defence has staged the first Innovation and Asymmetry Wargame to explore how emerging technologies might shape future military operations.

  • The Department of National Defence begins construction of the Land-Based Test Facility at Hartlen Point in Eastern Passage Canada 19 Jan 2026 -- Providing Canadian Armed Forces (CAF) members with the facilities and tools they need to support operations is critical to preparing personnel for the challenges of tomorrow. To help bring the Royal Canadian Navy's (RCN) new River-class destroyer (the River-class) into service and support them throughout their lifecycle, the Department of National Defence (DND) is building a Land-Based Test Facility (LBTF) on DND property at Hartlen Point in Eastern Passage, Nova Scotia.

  • INDIAN NAVY SIGNS CONTRACT FOR THREE 'BOTTOM OPENING NON-PROPELLED BARGES' WITH M/s SURYADIPTA PROJECTS PRIVATE LIMITED, THANE India PIB 19 Jan 2026 -- A contract for construction and delivery of 03 x 200T Bottom Opening Non-Propelled Barges has been concluded with M/s Suryadipta Projects Private Limited, Thane (MSME Shipyard) on 16 Jan 2026.

  • Pakistan Air Force contingent comprising F-16 Block-52 fighter aircraft, alongside dedicated air and ground crew, has arrived at King Abdulaziz Air Base, Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, to participate in the multinational aerial combat Exercise Spears of Victory-2026. ISPR Pakistan 19 Jan 2026

  • US delivers more F-35 jets to Israeli regime despite repeated Gaza ceasefire violations Press TV 19 Jan 2026 -- The United States has delivered a new shipment of F-35 stealth fighter jets to the Israeli regime despite its repeated Gaza ceasefire violations, according to the Israeli military, bringing Israel's total operational fleet of the advanced fifth-generation fighters to 48.
  • The Chief of the General Staff at the Home Front Command Israel Defense Forces 19 Jan 2026

  • Britain's fighter jet fleet strengthened in £205 million boost for British jobs UK MOD 19 Jan 2026 -- The UK has signed a £205 million contract extension with QinetiQ to strengthen the RAF's Typhoon fleet with technical and engineering support

  • Data on interceptions of aircraft completed near the Baltic States' borders on January 12 - 18, 2026 Lithuania MND 19 Jan 2026

  • Chinese FM spokesperson urges avoiding overstretching national security concept in response to Polish media reports on potential ban on Chinese EVs at military bases Global Times 19 Jan 2026

  • Drone strike against the Ukrainian Ports on the Danube Romania - Ministry of National Defence 19 Jan 2026 -- On Sunday, January 18th, at around 01:15, the Ministry of National Defence radar surveillance system identified a group of aerial drones of the Russian Federation operating in the Ukrainian airspace, approximately 10 km north of the border with Romania.
  • State Secretary Moldovan's visit to Chisinau: joint commitment to security and European integration of the Republic of Moldova Romania - Ministry of National Defence 19 Jan 2026 -- State Secretary for Defence Policy, Planning and International Relations, Sorin-Dan Moldovan, paid an official visit to the Republic of Moldova, at the invitation of his Moldovan counterpart, Ghenadie Cojocaru, between January 14 and 16, 2026.

  • Memorandum signed on Latvian-Canadian cooperation for military industry development Latvia MOD 19 Jan 2026 -- On Monday, January 19, at the Ministry of Defence of Latvia, State Secretary Aivars Puriņš and the Canadian Commercial Corporation signed a Memorandum of Understanding to strengthen cooperation between Latvia and Canada in the field of procurement.

  • Navy's new offshore patrol vessel Rajah Sulayman arrives in PH Philippine News Agency 19 Jan 2026 -- The Philippine Navy (PN) on Monday announced that one of the six offshore patrol vessels (OPVs) it ordered from South Korean shipbuilder HD Hyundai Heavy Industries (HHI), the prospective Rajah Sulayman (PS-20), arrived in the country on Jan. 17.

  • Secretary Rubio's Call with Azerbaijan Foreign Minister Bayramov US Dept. of State 19 Jan 2026
  • Secretary Rubio's Call with Saudi Foreign Minister Prince Faisal bin Farhan Al Saud US Dept. of State 19 Jan 2026

Defense Industry

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 19 January 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 19 Jan 2026

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • Gazans receive Trump's "Board of Peace" with deep skepticism People's Daily 19 Jan 2026 -- Residents of the Gaza Strip voiced concern and skepticism after U.S. President Donald Trump announced the creation of a new "Board of Peace" to govern Gaza, warning the initiative could impose external oversight without ensuring civilian protection or a lasting ceasefire.

  • Trump seeks 1 bln USD pledge for joining U.S. Peace Board: report People's Daily 19 Jan 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump plans to require countries to contribute at least 1 billion U.S. dollars to secure a permanent seat on the Trump-proposed "Board of Peace," Bloomberg reported on Saturday.

  • Kremlin Says Putin Received Invitation to Join Board of Peace for Gaza Sputnik 19 Jan 2026 -- Russian President Vladimir Putin has received an invitation to join the Board of Peace for Gaza, Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said on Monday.
  • Minsk Says Trump Proposes Belarus Become Founder of Gaza Board of Peace Sputnik 19 Jan 2026 -- US President Donald Trump sent Belarusian President Alexander Lukashenko an appeal proposing that Belarus become a founder of the Gaza Board of Peace, Belarusian Foreign Ministry spokesman Ruslan Varankov said on Monday.
  • HM the King Responds Positively to President Donald Trump's Invitation to Join Peace Board as Founding Member - Foreign Ministry Morocco Ministry of Foreign Affairs 19 Jan 2026

  • Blair distances himself from Trump's $1bn 'Board of Peace' fee Press TV 19 Jan 2026 -- Tony Blair, the former British prime minister appointed to the executive panel of US President Donald Trump's newly proposed "Board of Peace (BOP)", has sought to distance himself from a plan that would require countries to pay $1 billion for permanent membership.

  • Hamas blasts 'torture and brutality' against Palestinian prisoners in Israeli jails Press TV 19 Jan 2026 -- Palestinian resistance movement Hamas has issued a scathing denunciation of the Israeli regime over the treatment of Palestinian prisoners, describing their conditions as "inhumane" and warning that the world could not remain silent while such brutality continued.

  • Israel seeks to expropriate Palestinian-owned land in occupied East al-Quds Press TV 19 Jan 2026 -- Israeli authorities plan to seize 1,100 square meters of land on the southeastern outskirts of the occupied Old City of al-Quds, in the latest instance of the occupying Tel Aviv regime's theft of Palestinian land.

  • Food insecurity in Lebanon remains fragile despite signs of easing as the country enters the new year, new analysis shows WFP 19 Jan 2026 -- The Ministry of Agriculture, in collaboration with the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations and the United Nations World Food Programme (WFP), has launched the latest Integrated Food Security Phase Classification (IPC) analysis covering the period from November 2025 to July 2026, with technical support from the IPC Global Support Unit (GSU).
  • WFP scales up operations in Gaza as ceasefire passes 100 days WFP 19 Jan 2026 -- One hundred days into the ceasefire, the United Nations World Food Programme (WFP) has significantly expanded its life saving operations across the Gaza Strip, reaching more than one million people each month. While critical progress has been made in pushing back famine, the situation remains extremely fragile. Sustained access through all crossings, the flow of humanitarian and commercial goods, and the continuation of the ceasefire are essential to prevent Gaza from sliding back into catastrophic hunger.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Occupied Palestinian Territory, Mozambique, South Sudan, Syria, Ukraine UNOCHA 19 Jan 2026

  • Despite funding cuts, UNHCR responded to multiple complex emergencies last year UNHCR 19 Jan 2026 -- Even as severe funding cuts put further pressure on humanitarian resources, UNHCR responded last year to a range of highly complex new emergencies as well as intensifying protracted crises that forced millions of people from their homes, according to UNHCR's 2025 Impact Report: Response to New Emergencies and Protracted Crises, released today.
  • Rising hunger and displacement pose growing economic risk, UN tells Davos UN News 19 Jan 2026 -- As global leaders gather at the World Economic Forum in Davos this week, UN agencies are warning that rising hunger and displacement are not only humanitarian emergencies but growing threats to global economic stability.

  • Several killed as explosion rips through Kabul's high-security district Press TV 19 Jan 2026 -- A powerful explosion rocked a heavily fortified district of Kabul on Monday, leaving several people dead and injured, according to a Taliban Interior Ministry spokesperson.

  • Yemen: Children are dying and it's going to get worse, warns aid veteran UN News 19 Jan 2026 -- In Yemen, renewed political instability and economic woes linked to the war threaten to complicate the already difficult task of helping vulnerable people suffering from deepening hunger, illness and displacement, the UN's top aid official there said on Monday.

  • Syria: Statement by the High Representative / Vice President Kaja Kallas on the ceasefire European External Action Service (EEAS) 19 Jan 2026 -- The ceasefire between Syrian interim authorities and SDF is a vital step to prevent the country from sliding back into turmoil and to consolidate state functions.
  • Secretary-General of the Organization of Islamic Cooperation Welcomes the Ceasefire Agreement in Syria Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 19 Jan 2026 -- The Secretary-General of the Organization of Islamic Cooperation, Mr
  • "The era of terrorism in our region is over once and for all" Presidency of The Republic of Turkey 19 Jan 2026 -- Delivering remarks following the Presidential Cabinet Meeting, President Erdoğan said: "Syria's fertile lands have had enough of suffering, bloodshed, and tears. What must be done from now on is clear. Foot-dragging, stalling or hiding behind excuses will not help anyone. The era of terrorism in our region is over once and for all. The requirements of the ceasefire and full integration agreement must be implemented swiftly, and all must avoid yet another miscalculation."

  • Maria Malmer Stenergard in talks with Moroccan Minister of Foreign Affairs Government Offices of Sweden 19 Jan 2026 -- On 19 January, Minister for Foreign Affairs Maria Malmer Stenergard spoke on the phone with Nasser Bourita, Morocco's Minister of Foreign Affairs, African Cooperation and Moroccan Expatriates.
  • Statement from the Ministry for Foreign Affairs on the UN process on Western Sahara Government Offices of Sweden 19 Jan 2026 -- "Sweden has repeatedly pointed to the importance of a just, lasting and mutually agreed solution to the Western Sahara issue, in accordance with UN Security Council resolutions and the Charter of the United Nations."

  • Sudan: Atrocities 'repeated town by town', ICC prosecutor tells UN Security Council UN News 19 Jan 2026 -- Atrocities in Sudan's Darfur region are spreading from town to town in an organized campaign of violence that includes mass executions, rape and ethnic targeting, amounting to war crimes and crimes against humanity, the prosecutor of the International Criminal Court told the UN Security Council on Monday.
  • Rapid Support Forces Abuses Repeated "Town after Town" in Darfur, International Criminal Court Deputy Prosecutor Tells Security Council, Citing Mass Crimes UN Security Council 19 Jan 2026 -- The gruesome crisis in Sudan has "darkened even further" in the last six months, the Deputy Prosecutor of the International Criminal Court said today, citing a pattern of large-scale crimes against humanity that is being repeated "in town after town" across the country's Darfur region.
  • The Sudanese people deserve justice, and we welcome the ongoing role of the ICC in achieving that goal: UK statement at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 19 Jan 2026 -- Statement by Legal Adviser Colin McIntyre at the UN Security Council meeting on the ICC's work in Sudan.

News Reports

  • Judge bars U.S. immigration agents from using force against peaceful Minnesota protesters People's Daily 19 Jan 2026 -- A U.S. federal judge on Friday ordered immigration agents to stop using pepper spray, making arrests, or detaining people who are peacefully protesting immigration enforcement operations in Minnesota.
  • U.S. Minneapolis shooting continues to fuel debate as protests, partisan clash intensify People's Daily 19 Jan 2026 -- The fatal shooting of a U.S. woman by an Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agent in Minneapolis has continued to stoke tensions locally and nationwide, as state and federal officials sharply clash over how the incident unfolded and should be handled.
  • US Justice Department refuses probe into killing of Minneapolis mother Press TV 19 Jan 2026 -- The US Justice Department has confirmed it is not opening a criminal or civil rights investigation into the fatal shooting of a woman by an Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agent in Minneapolis.

  • Meeting with permanent members of the Security Council President of Russia 19 Jan 2026 -- Vladimir Putin chaired, via videoconference, a briefing session with permanent members of the Security Council.
  • Father Ill, Son Injured: Car Crash Further Clouds Chechnya's Future RFE/RL 19 Jan 2026 -- A deadly, multiple-car accident on the streets of Chechnya's capital has added to uncertainty about the future in the southern Russian region, the volatile site of two wars and an Islamist insurgency since the Soviet collapse.
  • Press release on entry into force of the Agreement between the Government of the Russian Federation and the Government of the Republic of the Union of Myanmar on the Mutual Cancellation of Visa Requirements for Citizens of the Russian Federation and Citizens of the Republic of the Union of Myanmar Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 19 Jan 2026

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun's Regular Press Conference on January 19, 2026 PRC MofA 19 Jan 2026
  • China's Diplomatic Pressure Looms Over Case Against Xinjiang Activists In Kazakhstan RFE/RL 19 Jan 2026 -- Documents seen by RFE/RL highlight Chinese government pressure behind the prosecution of 19 activists in Kazakhstan who organized demonstrations against China's mass internment camps for Uyghurs and other Muslim minorities in Xinjiang.
  • China's GDP expands 5% in 2025, meeting the annual growth target Global Times 19 Jan 2026 -- China's GDP grew at 5 percent year-on-year to 140.19 trillion yuan ($20.13 trillion) in 2025, data from the National Bureau of Statistics (NBS) showed on Monday, meeting the annual growth target of around 5 percent and surpassing the 140-trillion-yuan threshold for the first time. Observers said that the upbeat data underscored the resilience and vitality of the world's second-largest economy despite multiple downward pressures, and such growth momentum will continue to position China as a beacon of stability and the locomotive of the world economy.
  • China's Shenzhou-20 return capsule touches down on Earth Global Times 19 Jan 2026 -- The return capsule of the Shenzhou-20 spaceship, carrying no astronauts, touched down at the Dongfeng landing site in north China's Inner Mongolia Autonomous Region at 9:34 a.m. (Beijing Time) on Monday, according to the China Manned Space Agency (CMSA).
  • China's Shenzhou-20 return capsule touches down on Earth People's Daily 19 Jan 2026 -- The return capsule of the Shenzhou-20 spaceship, carrying no astronauts, touched down at the Dongfeng landing site in north China's Inner Mongolia Autonomous Region at 9:34 a.m. (Beijing Time) on Monday, according to the China Manned Space Agency (CMSA).
  • PhilSA: Chinese rocket debris may have fallen in PH waters Philippine News Agency 19 Jan 2026 -- Debris from a Chinese rocket launched on Monday could have fallen within the country's archipelagic waters, the Philippine Space Agency (PhilSA) said.

  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 19 Jan 2026

  • Netanyahu Urges Trump Not to Attack Iran Due to Israel's Lack of Defense Ability - Reports Sputnik 19 Jan 2026 -- US President Donald Trump's decision not to strike Iran this week may have been influenced by a warning from his ally, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, who pointed out Israel's lack of preparedness to defend itself in the event of a retaliatory strike from Tehran, Axios reported citing US and Israeli officials.
  • Pezeshkian calls recent violent events in Iran part of 'failed' US-Israeli war Press TV 19 Jan 2026 -- President Masoud Pezeshkian referred to the recent violent events in Iran as "the continuation of the failed and unsuccessful" campaign by the United States and the Zionist regime in the 12-day war against the Islamic Republic.
  • Recent terrorist war on Iran similar to Israel's pager attack on Lebanon: Qalibaf Press TV 19 Jan 2026 -- Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad-Baqer Qalibaf says the recent "Daesh-style terrorist war" against the country had similar objectives to Israel's terrorist pager attack on Lebanon in September 2024.
  • Iran Mulls Return Of Internet As Extent Of Deadly Crackdown Slowly Leaks Out RFE/RL 19 Jan 2026 -- Iran said it is considering lifting its Internet blackout this week as details of a brutal crackdown on antiestablishment protests where thousands are reported dead continue to leak out of the country.
  • Iranians Living Under Virtual Lockdown After Deadly Protests RFE/RL 19 Jan 2026 -- The usually bustling streets of Iran's capital are largely empty as security forces reassert their control after crushing unprecedented protests against the country's clerical rulers.
  • Iran Foreign Ministry condemns Argentina's unfounded accusations against IRGC Press TV 19 Jan 2026 -- The Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Islamic Republic of Iran has condemned Argentina's recent decision to accuse a section of the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC), calling it unjustified and harmful to national security.
  • World Economic Forum revokes Iran FM's Davos invitation 'under US-Israeli pressure' Press TV 19 Jan 2026 -- The World Economic Forum (WEF) has rescinded its invitation to Iran's Foreign Minister, Abbas Araghchi, to attend the Davos summit in Switzerland, following Tehran's response to US- and Israeli-backed riots which erupted in the country earlier this month.
  • Alex Vatanka: Khamenei's Iran Is A 'Sinking Ship' That Must Compromise To Survive RFE/RL 19 Jan 2026 -- Iran's clerical rulers resorted to unprecedented brute force to put down weekslong antiestablishment protests that posed the biggest threat to their rule in decades.
  • US-Iran Tensions High As Tehran Revives Hostile Rhetoric, Threats Of New Crackdown RFE/RL 19 Jan 2026 -- US-Iranian tensions remained high as Tehran's hard-line rulers renewed their hostile rhetoric and threatened to hand out the "severest punishments" - including potentially executions - against anti-government protesters.

  • PM call with Prime Minister Meloni of Italy and Prime Minister Carney of Canada: 19 January 2026 Gov.UK 19 Jan 2026

  • Takaichi to dissolve lower house for snap election; Japan PM tries to shift focus, alleviate stress from diplomatic difficulties with Beijing: experts Global Times 19 Jan 2026 -- Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi announced on Monday that she will dissolve the House of Representatives on January 23, the day the ordinary Diet session convenes, for a snap general election, according to media reports. Japanese media described the move as "an unusually early election at an unusual time." Besides the weak position of the ruling party, Japanese media noted that factors behind the decision also included strained China-Japan relations following her Taiwan-related remarks.
  • Japan-EU Foreign Ministers' Telephone Meeting Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 19 Jan 2026

  • EU inks trade deal with Mercosur despite farmers' fierce opposition; 'huge amount' of agricultural products likely to be redirected to bloc Global Times 19 Jan 2026 -- The EU on Saturday formally signed a free trade agreement with the Southern Common Market (Mercosur), despite facing fierce opposition from EU farmers. Amid some changes in the international market, the deal, once it enters into force, could redirect a huge number of agricultural products from South America to the EU market, a senior Chinese industry expert who requested anonymity told the Global Times on Sunday.

  • Rumen Radev: We Have a Battle Ahead for the Future of the Fatherland and I Believe We Will Fight It Together with All of You - the Worthy, the Inspired and the Irreconcilable President of the Republic of Bulgaria 19 Jan 2026 -- In an address to the Bulgarian people, the president announced that he would submit his resignation from office tomorrow

  • Minister Sidhu concludes productive trip to Gulf region in United Arab Emirates Canada 19 Jan 2026 -- The Honourable Maninder Sidhu, Minister of International Trade, concluded, on January 13, 2026, his trip to the Gulf region with a visit to the United Arab Emirates (UAE). The Minister was accompanied on the trip by a high-level Canadian business delegation.

  • Marcos announces major Malampaya gas find, boosts energy security Philippine News Agency 19 Jan 2026 -- President Ferdinand R. Marcos Jr. on Monday announced a major natural gas discovery near the Malampaya field, calling it the country's first significant find in more than a decade and a critical boost to the Philippines' energy security.

  • Government receives Qatar's Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs Sheikh Mohammed Al Thani Government Offices of Sweden 19 Jan 2026 -- On Monday 19 January, Prime Minister Ulf Kristersson, Minister for Foreign Affairs Maria Malmer Stenergard and Minister for Defence Pål Jonson received Qatar's Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman Al Thani for bilateral meetings in Stockholm.

  • World News in Brief: Food insecurity in Lebanon, Libya migrants freed, UNHCR tackles multiple emergencies - despite cuts UN News 19 Jan 2026 -- Since 2026, nearly 17 percent of Lebanon's population has been living in a state of major food insecurity - a situation that is likely to worsen in the coming year.
